abstract.para: &dlm; depends on &corosync;. If &corosync; stops, the &dlm; resource assumes a split-brain scenario and triggers a fencing operation. Stopping the cluster services with crm cluster stop doesn't usually cause this problem because the &dlm; resource also stops. However, if the cluster is in maintenance mode, the &dlm; resource keeps running even when the cluster services are offline. Therefore, if you need to put the cluster into maintenance mode and then stop the cluster services, you must first stop &dlm; and any resources that depend on it.

topic.para: You can perform this procedure on any node in the cluster.

step.para: Log in either as the &rootuser; user or as a user with sudo privileges.
Stop &dlm; and any &dlm;-dependent resources. You can stop multiple resources at once:
The --wait option makes sure the resources have really stopped before the command returns.
Check the status of the cluster:
&dlm; and any &dlm;-dependent resources should have the status Stopped.
